page.settings.title: Settings
toast.loading.title: Loading
param.server.ctx_size.name: Context window size
param.server.ctx_size.desc: --ctx-size: Set the context window size available to the model (tokens). The context must hold both the prompt and subsequently generated tokens. Larger values improve long chat/document capability, but KV cache RAM/VRAM usage grows accordingly, and prefill/decode compute may also increase. Set to 0 to read the default context size from the model file metadata.
param.server.flash_attn.name: Flash Attention
param.server.flash_attn.desc: --flash-attn: Control whether to enable Flash Attention (fused attention optimization): on = force enable, off = force disable, auto = auto-detect backend capability. Enabling usually reduces attention memory-bandwidth overhead and improves throughput (especially with long contexts or large-batch prefill), and may also reduce VRAM usage; but on some hardware/driver/backend combinations it can regress or be unstable. If you see abnormal speed, unusually high VRAM peaks, or need A/B testing, switch to off; when unsure, use auto.
param.server.no_mmap.name: Disable mmap loading
param.server.no_mmap.desc: --no-mmap: When enabled, the model is loaded without memory mapping (mmap) and weights are read into memory normally. mmap usually reduces copying at startup and lets the OS page data on demand, resulting in faster startup and better memory behavior. Disabling mmap can avoid mapping failures, SIGBUS, or stalls in some file systems / network drives / permission-limited / container environments, but often increases one-time memory usage and load time. Toggle this option when loading is slow, fails, or is unstable to find a more stable/faster combination.
param.server.mlock.name: Lock model in RAM
param.server.mlock.desc: --mlock: Lock model weights in physical memory (RAM) to reduce the risk of occasional slowdowns during inference caused by swapping/compression, improving latency stability. This can significantly increase required RAM and may require elevated permissions/quotas on some systems; if memory is insufficient it may prevent startup or cause system memory pressure.
param.server.embedding.name: Embedding mode
param.server.embedding.desc: --embedding: Enable embeddings, which encodes input text into vector representations for semantic search, clustering, similarity matching, and RAG. This mode is typically used with embedding-specific models or models that support embedding outputs. Unlike text generation, it focuses on vector output and has different throughput/memory characteristics. Enable only when you need an embeddings API or vector output.
param.server.rerank.name: Rerank mode
param.server.rerank.desc: --rerank: Enable reranking, which scores the relevance of 'query + candidate documents/passages' and sorts them by score. Commonly used as a rerank stage in RAG to improve citation quality. This mode is typically intended for rerank-specific models (often cross-encoder style) and follows a different inference path than standard generation models. It adds scoring compute overhead but can significantly improve ranking quality. Enable only when you need a rerank API/feature.
param.server.temp.name: Temperature
param.server.temp.desc: --temp: Controls sampling randomness. Lower values produce more deterministic output, closer to high-probability tokens (more conservative, less diverse but possibly more rigid); higher values flatten the distribution for more diverse/creative output but with higher risk of drifting or unreliable content. Common range is about 0.1–1.2; setting 0 typically approaches greedy/deterministic decoding (behavior depends on implementation).
param.server.samplers.name: Sampler chain (ordered multi-select)
param.server.samplers.desc: --samplers: Compose llama.cpp sampler stages in order. Checking an item enables it; the order shown on the right is the real execution order. The final value is joined with semicolons and passed to --samplers.
param.server.top_p.name: Top-P (nucleus sampling)
param.server.top_p.desc: --top-p: Nucleus sampling (Top-P): at each step, sample only from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability reaches P. Smaller P narrows candidates and stabilizes output; larger P increases variety. P = 1.0 disables Top-P truncation.
param.server.top_k.name: Top-K
param.server.top_k.desc: --top-k: Top-K sampling: at each step, sample only from the K most probable tokens. Smaller K is more conservative; larger K is more diverse. Too small can lead to repetitive phrasing; too large can increase drift. K = 0 disables Top-K truncation.
param.server.min_p.name: Min-P
param.server.min_p.desc: --min-p: Minimum probability threshold (Min-P): keep only tokens whose probability is at least (probability of the most likely token × P). This filters out extremely low-probability tail tokens to reduce gibberish/garbage tokens or extreme jumps. Larger P filters more aggressively and stabilizes output but can become overly conservative. P = 0.0 disables Min-P.
param.server.top_n_sigma.name: Top-N Sigma
param.server.top_n_sigma.desc: --top-nsigma: Top-N Sigma sampling keeps only tokens whose logits are above the threshold of mean + N × standard deviation, dynamically trimming the candidate set based on how spread out the distribution is. Smaller N filters more aggressively and makes output more concentrated; larger N keeps more candidates. Set to -1.0 to disable. It is often combined with Top-K, Top-P, and Min-P to further suppress low-quality tail candidates.
param.server.presence_penalty.name: Presence penalty
param.server.presence_penalty.desc: --presence-penalty: Apply a penalty to any token that has already appeared in the current context to encourage introducing new words/new information and reduce repeated mentions. Higher values avoid repetition and topic loops more strongly, but too high can cause erratic wording or off-topic output. 0.0 disables.
param.server.repeat_penalty.name: Repeat penalty
param.server.repeat_penalty.desc: --repeat-penalty: Repeat penalty multiplier: reduces the probability of sampling tokens that have appeared recently to suppress 'looping' behavior. 1.0 means no penalty; values above 1.0 increase suppression (commonly 1.05–1.2). Too high can harm fluency or force unnatural wording.
param.server.frequency_penalty.name: Frequency penalty
param.server.frequency_penalty.desc: --frequency-penalty: Apply a penalty based on how often a token appears in the current context: more frequent tokens get stronger penalties to reduce high-frequency repetition and template-like phrasing. Unlike presence penalty, it accumulates with count. Higher values suppress repetition more, but too high can hurt coherence. 0.0 disables.
param.server.dry_multiplier.name: DRY multiplier
param.server.dry_multiplier.desc: --dry-multiplier: Multiplier for DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) sampling, used to apply additional penalties when the model starts extending repeated phrases or patterns. Higher values suppress repetitive continuations more strongly and can reduce looping over similar wording, sentences, or paragraph structures; values that are too high may hurt natural flow. Set to 0.0 to disable DRY sampling.
param.server.dry_base.name: DRY base
param.server.dry_base.desc: --dry-base: Exponential base used by DRY sampling to control how quickly the penalty grows once a repeated sequence exceeds the allowed length. Larger values make long repetitions get penalized much faster, while smaller values grow more gently. This is usually tuned together with the DRY multiplier, and the default 1.75 is a reasonable starting point for most cases.
param.server.dry_allowed_length.name: DRY allowed length
param.server.dry_allowed_length.desc: In DRY sampling, penalties only start growing exponentially once the repeated span becomes longer than this threshold. Smaller values begin suppressing repeated continuations earlier; larger values allow short repeated fragments to pass more naturally. The default is typically 2.
param.server.dry_penalty_last_n.name: DRY scan window
param.server.dry_penalty_last_n.desc: How many recent tokens to scan when detecting repeated spans for DRY sampling. Larger values look for repetition across a wider context window but also apply the constraint more broadly; 0 disables it, and -1 usually means the full context length.
param.server.dry_sequence_breakers.name: DRY sequence breakers
param.server.dry_sequence_breakers.desc: A JSON array of strings that break repetition matching for DRY sampling, preventing penalties from continuing across separators such as newlines, colons, or quotes. This field only accepts a JSON string array, for example ["\n", ":", "\"", "*"].
param.server.batch_size.name: Batch size
param.server.batch_size.desc: --batch-size: Logical batch size: mainly affects how many tokens can be processed in parallel during the prompt prefill stage. Increasing it often improves throughput and speeds up long prompts, but increases peak VRAM/RAM usage and can trigger OOM or performance regressions on some backends. This value is more like a logical ceiling; actual execution also depends on micro-batch size, backend implementation, and available VRAM.
param.server.ubatch_size.name: Micro-batch size (uBatch)
param.server.ubatch_size.desc: --ubatch-size: Physical micro-batch size: splits a large logical batch into smaller micro-batches to balance throughput and peak VRAM usage. Larger values reduce splitting overhead and speed up prefill but increase VRAM peaks; smaller values are more VRAM-friendly and stable but may reduce throughput. When VRAM is tight or concurrency is high, lowering this value can help avoid OOM.
param.server.parallel.name: Parallel slots
param.server.parallel.desc: --parallel: Number of parallel slots the server can handle concurrently, effectively limiting concurrent sessions. Higher values increase concurrency, but each slot consumes KV cache and runtime resources, increasing RAM/VRAM usage and potentially reducing per-request throughput or increasing latency. -1 means auto (typically based on context size and available resources). Increase for short contexts/smaller models with high concurrency needs; be conservative for long contexts or VRAM-constrained setups.
param.server.port.name: Port
param.server.port.desc: --port: TCP port the llama-server listens on. Leave at 0 to let the application auto-assign an available port. Set to a specific value (e.g., 8080) to bind to a fixed port.
param.server.cache_ram.name: KV cache memory limit
param.server.cache_ram.desc: --cache-ram: Maximum KV cache RAM/VRAM budget (MiB). KV cache stores attention K/V states and is a primary memory consumer for long contexts and high concurrency. Higher limits support longer contexts or more slots, but increase memory usage. -1 means no limit. 0 disables KV cache (usually makes autoregressive generation unusable or extremely slow; only for special tests).
param.server.kv_unified.name: Unified KV buffer
param.server.kv_unified.desc: --kv-unified: Use a unified/shared KV buffer management strategy across sequences/sessions to reuse and allocate KV memory more efficiently under concurrency. This often reduces fragmentation and peak usage and improves stability, but in extreme cases can affect throughput or memory layout behavior. When slots (parallel) are in auto mode, this is often enabled by default for more stable resource management.
param.server.cache_type_k.name: KV cache type (K)
param.server.cache_type_k.desc: --cache-type-k: Data type (precision/quantization) for K vectors in the KV cache. Higher precision (e.g., f32) uses more VRAM and is usually more stable. Lower precision or quantization (e.g., q8/q5/q4/iq4) can significantly reduce VRAM usage and may improve speed in bandwidth-bound scenarios, but can introduce minor quality changes or numerical stability differences; support varies by backend. Options: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl, q5_0, q5_1.
param.server.cache_type_v.name: KV cache type (V)
param.server.cache_type_v.desc: --cache-type-v: Data type (precision/quantization) for V vectors in the KV cache. Similar to K type, it mainly affects KV cache VRAM usage and bandwidth. Lower precision/quantization can reduce memory usage and increase supported context length or concurrency, but may cause minor quality changes or instability in rare environments. Try lower precision when VRAM is tight or you need longer context/higher concurrency, and compare results. Options: f32, f16, bf16, q8_0, q4_0, q4_1, iq4_nl, q5_0, q5_1.
param.server.threads.name: Threads
param.server.threads.desc: --threads: Number of CPU threads used during inference (affects CPU compute and scheduling in some backends). More threads is not always faster: too many can cause contention, cache thrashing, or resource starvation, increasing latency. -1 means auto-detect (typically based on physical cores or system recommendations). For CPU inference or stable latency, test around the number of physical cores to find the best value.
param.server.seed.name: Random seed
param.server.seed.desc: --seed: Random number generator seed for sampling reproducibility: with the same model, prompt, and sampling parameters, a fixed seed usually yields identical or highly similar outputs, useful for regression tests and reproducing issues. -1 uses a random seed per start/request for more diversity.
param.server.directio.name: Direct I/O
param.server.directio.desc: -ndio / -dio: Enable Direct I/O, which affects model loading speed. Enabled by default. llama.cpp's implementation may be problematic; if you encounter loading issues, disable it.
param.server.no_jinja.name: Disable Jinja
param.server.no_jinja.desc: --no-jinja: Disable the Jinja template engine for chat. Jinja is enabled by default, so this option is false by default.
param.server.chat_template.name: Built-in chat template
param.server.chat_template.desc: --chat-template: Generally do not use this unless your GGUF model does not include a built-in chat template. If you do use it, set 'Disable Jinja' to 1; otherwise, current llama.cpp will not apply the built-in template. Default is empty, meaning use the model's built-in template. If the built-in template is inaccurate, try overriding it here. ⚠ If both --chat-template and --chat-template-file are set, the one appearing later overrides the earlier.
param.server.context_shift.name: Context shift
param.server.context_shift.desc: --context-shift: Disabled by default. Enable the KV cache sliding-window mechanism (also called 'context shift' or 'context compression'). When the context exceeds --ctx-size, the system automatically discards the oldest tokens (from the left) and keeps the most recent tokens, instead of failing with an error.
param.server.no_webui.name: Disable webui
param.server.no_webui.desc: --no-webui: Since it's the web UI that comes with llama.cpp, it generally doesn't need to be enabled.
param.server.json_schema.name: JSON Schema
param.server.json_schema.desc: --json-schema / -j: JSON Schema to constrain model output to a structured format. The schema is converted to GBNF grammar at startup. WARNING: Complex schemas produce extremely long CLI commands that may exceed the OS command length limit (8191 chars on Windows). For large schemas, use --json-schema-file instead. Also supported per-request via the API (response_format.json_schema). If both --json-schema and --json-schema-file are set, the later one wins. ⚠ If you do not understand what this does, do not modify it.
param.server.json_schema_file.name: JSON Schema file
param.server.json_schema_file.desc: --json-schema-file / -jf: Path to a JSON Schema file on disk. Recommended over --json-schema for complex or lengthy schemas to avoid OS CLI length limits. Use the file browser to select a .json file. Also supported per-request via API (response_format.json_schema). If both --json-schema-file and --json-schema are set, the later one wins. ⚠ If you do not understand what this does, do not modify it.
param.server.chat_template_kwargs.name: Chat template kwargs
param.server.chat_template_kwargs.desc: --chat-template-kwargs: Additional key-value pairs to pass to the Jinja chat template engine. Must be valid JSON object, e.g. {"key1":"value1"}. Click the code button to edit. For per-model defaults, use the Kwargs panel in Model Details instead — it has higher priority than this CLI parameter. Priority (lowest → highest): CLI startup args → API request body chat_template_kwargs → model detail Kwargs settings (model-chat-template-kwargs.json). The last source wins per key. ⚠ If you do not understand what this does, do not modify it.
modal.json_editor.title: Edit JSON
modal.json_editor.desc: Enter JSON content below. Invalid JSON will not be saved.
param.server.reasoning_format.name: Reasoning Format
param.server.reasoning_format.desc: --reasoning-format: controls whether thought tags are allowed and/or extracted from the response, and in which format they're returned; one of: - none: leaves thoughts unparsed in message.content; - deepseek: puts thoughts in message.reasoning_content; - deepseek-legacy: keeps <think> tags in message.content while also populating message.reasoning_content(default: auto)
param.server.reasoning_budget.name: Reasoning Budget
param.server.reasoning_budget.desc: --reasoning-budget: Controls the allowed amount of reasoning; -1 indicates an unlimited reasoning budget, or 0 indicates disabling thinking. Any other value specifies the maximum length of the reasoning chain, though this may not always be fully effective (default: -1).
param.server.no_mmproj_offload.name: no mmproj offload
param.server.no_mmproj_offload.desc: --no-mmproj-offload: Offload mmproj projection to CPU. You can enable this option when GPU VRAM is insufficient, but CPU image processing performance is typically inferior to that of the GPU.
param.server.mmproj.name: Multimodal projector (mmproj)
param.server.mmproj.desc: --mmproj: Path to a multimodal projector .gguf file. Required for vision/language models (e.g. LLaVA, Qwen-VL). If not set, the server will auto-detect a mmproj file next to the model. Set this manually to override the auto-detected file. Use the file browser to select a .gguf file. ⚠ If you do not understand what this does, do not modify it.
param.server.image_min_tokens.name: Image min tokens
param.server.image_min_tokens.desc: --image-min-tokens: Minimum number of tokens each image can take, only used by vision models with dynamic resolution. When not set, the value is read from the model file. If you don't know what this does, leave it alone.
param.server.image_max_tokens.name: Image max tokens
param.server.image_max_tokens.desc: --image-max-tokens: Maximum number of tokens each image can take, only used by vision models with dynamic resolution. When not set, the value is read from the model file. If you don't know what this does, leave it alone.
param.server.ctx_checkpoints.name: CTX Checkpoints
param.server.ctx_checkpoints.desc: max number of context checkpoints to create per slot. If there is insufficient video memory (some models may have vram leak issues, such as Gemma-4, see issue #21690 in llama.cpp), please reduce this value or set it to 0. (default: 8)
param.server.checkpoint_every_nt.name: Checkpoint interval
param.server.checkpoint_every_nt.desc: --checkpoint-every-n-tokens / -cpent: Create a context checkpoint every N tokens during prompt prefill. -1 to disable (server default: 8192). Useful for long prompts so the slot can resume from a checkpoint if interrupted. Works together with --ctx-checkpoints: this controls how often to create checkpoints, while --ctx-checkpoints limits how many are kept per slot. ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of this parameter, do not modify it.
param.server.no_cache_idle_slots.name: Disable idle slot cache
param.server.no_cache_idle_slots.desc: --no-cache-idle-slots: By default, when you send a new request, the server automatically saves the KV cache of all currently idle slots to RAM and clears their GPU memory to free up VRAM. If you tick this box, this automatic saving behavior is turned off — idle slots will keep their KV cache in VRAM. Only tick this if you are experiencing crashes or weird behavior caused by the idle slot caching feature. WARNING: turning this off may increase VRAM usage! ⚠ If you do not understand what this does, do not tick it.
param.server.swa_full.name: Full-size SWA cache
param.server.swa_full.desc: --swa-full: Use full-size Sliding Window Attention (SWA) KV cache instead of the default window-sized cache. Only affects models that use sliding window attention (e.g. Mistral, Gemma-2, Qwen, etc.). When enabled, context checkpoints work correctly on SWA layers. WARNING: significantly increases KV cache memory usage! ⚠ If you do not understand what this does, do not modify it.
param.server.split_mode.name: Split Mode
param.server.split_mode.desc: Only required for multiple GPUs. Split mode: layer (default recommended, suitable for most scenarios and mixed GPU models), row (lower performance version of tensor), tensor (new llama.cpp feature, requires identical GPU models for optimal performance).
param.server.cpu_moe.name: CPU MoE
param.server.cpu_moe.desc: --cpu-moe: Keep all Mixture of Experts (MoE) weights in CPU memory instead of offloading to GPU. Enable this when GPU VRAM is insufficient for the MoE expert weights, forcing all MoE computation onto the CPU. This significantly increases CPU memory usage and may reduce inference speed, but avoids OOM errors caused by insufficient VRAM.
param.server.numa.name: NUMA mode
param.server.numa.desc: --numa: Attempt optimizations that help on some NUMA systems. distribute: spread execution evenly over all nodes. isolate: only spawn threads on CPUs on the node that execution started on. numactl: use the CPU map provided by numactl. If run without this previously, it is recommended to drop the system page cache before using this.
param.server.n_cpu_moe.name: N CPU MoE
param.server.n_cpu_moe.desc: --n-cpu-moe N: Keep the Mixture of Experts (MoE) weights of the first N layers in the CPU, letting remaining layers offload to GPU normally. A finer-grained alternative to --cpu-moe when GPU VRAM is insufficient for all MoE layers but you don't want to offload all of them to CPU.
param.server.spec_draft_p_min.name: Draft min probability
param.server.spec_draft_p_min.desc: --spec-draft-p-min / --draft-p-min P: Minimum speculative decoding probability (greedy) (default: 0.75). ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_n_max.name: Max draft tokens
param.server.spec_draft_n_max.desc: --spec-draft-n-max: Maximum number of tokens to draft per speculative decoding step (default: 16). 💡 For Qwen3.5 models, setting this value to 2 works better, no other parameters needed. ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_n_min.name: Min draft tokens
param.server.spec_draft_n_min.desc: --spec-draft-n-min: Minimum number of draft tokens to use (default: 0). ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_p_split.name: Draft split probability
param.server.spec_draft_p_split.desc: --spec-draft-p-split / --draft-p-split: Speculative decoding split probability (default: 0.10). ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_model.name: Draft model path
param.server.spec_draft_model.desc: --spec-draft-model / --model-draft: Path to the draft model file. Leave empty for MTP to use the target model's MTP head automatically. ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_ngl.name: Draft model GPU layers
param.server.spec_draft_ngl.desc: --spec-draft-ngl / --n-gpu-layers-draft: Maximum layers to offload to VRAM for the draft model. Options: -1=auto (default), auto=automatic, all=all layers, or specify a number. ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_device.name: Draft model devices
param.server.spec_draft_device.desc: --spec-draft-device / --device-draft: Comma-separated list of GPU devices for the draft model. Leave empty to use the same devices as the target model. ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_threads.name: Draft model threads
param.server.spec_draft_threads.desc: --spec-draft-threads / --threads-draft: Number of threads for draft model generation. -1 uses the same as the target model threads. ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_draft_type_k.name: Draft model K cache type
param.server.spec_draft_type_k.desc: --spec-draft-type-k / -ctkd / --cache-type-k-draft: KV cache data type for K for the draft model. Default: f16. Applies only when speculative decoding is enabled.
param.server.spec_draft_type_v.name: Draft model V cache type
param.server.spec_draft_type_v.desc: --spec-draft-type-v / -ctvd / --cache-type-v-draft: KV cache data type for V for the draft model. Default: f16. Applies only when speculative decoding is enabled.
param.server.fit.name: Fit to device memory
param.server.fit.desc: --fit: Whether to automatically adjust unset arguments to fit within available device memory: on = auto-adjust (default), off = do not auto-adjust. When enabled, the system adjusts context size, batch size, and other parameters based on available VRAM to avoid OOM; when disabled, it uses explicitly set values and fails if VRAM is exceeded.
param.server.ngl.name: GPU layers
param.server.ngl.desc: --gpu-layers / -ngl: Maximum number of layers to store in VRAM. Options: -1 = let fit calculate the optimal value based on available VRAM (default), auto = let llama.cpp decide automatically, all = offload all layers to GPU, or specify an exact layer count.
param.server.spec_type.name: Speculative decoding type
param.server.spec_type.desc: --spec-type: Type of speculative decoding to use (default: none). Options: none=disabled, draft-simple=standalone draft model, draft-eagle3=Eagle3 draft model, draft-mtp=Multi-Token Prediction (MTP, requires model support). ⚠ If you do not understand the specific meaning of these parameters, do not use them. These parameters require considerable expertise and sufficient patience to test.
param.server.spec_type_mtp.name: MTP Speculative Decoding
param.server.spec_type_mtp.desc: --spec-type draft-mtp: Enable Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) speculative decoding. The model must support MTP architecture (e.g., Qwen3). Requires --spec-draft-model to be set to the MTP donor model path.
param.server.spec_type_ngram_mod.name: Ngram-mod Speculative Decoding
param.server.spec_type_ngram_mod.desc: --spec-type ngram-mod: Enable ngram-mod speculative decoding. Uses a hash pool (shared across server slots) to map n-gram hashes to next tokens (~16 MB, constant memory).
param.server.spec_ngram_mod_n_match.name: Ngram-mod Lookup Length
param.server.spec_ngram_mod_n_match.desc: --spec-ngram-mod-n-match N: ngram-mod lookup length, size of the n-gram to hash (default: 24, range: 1-1024)
param.server.spec_ngram_mod_n_min.name: Ngram-mod Min Draft Length
param.server.spec_ngram_mod_n_min.desc: --spec-ngram-mod-n-min N: minimum draft tokens (default: 48, range: 0-1024). Drafts shorter than this are discarded.
param.server.spec_ngram_mod_n_max.name: Ngram-mod Max Draft Length
param.server.spec_ngram_mod_n_max.desc: --spec-ngram-mod-n-max N: maximum draft tokens (default: 64, range: 0-1024). Hash chain stops at this length.
param.server.spec_type_ngram_map_k4v.des: --spec-type ngram-map-k4v: Enable ngram-map-k4v speculative decoding. Looks for the current n-gram key in token history, tracks up to 4 m-gram values per key. Uses the most frequent value as draft if it significantly outweighs alternatives.
param.server.spec_ngram_map_k4v_size_n.d: --spec-ngram-map-k4v-size-n N: length of lookup key n-gram (default: 12, range: 1-1024)
param.server.spec_ngram_map_k4v_size_m.d: --spec-ngram-map-k4v-size-m N: length of draft m-gram (default: 48, range: 1-1024)
param.server.spec_ngram_map_k4v_min_hits: --spec-ngram-map-k4v-min-hits N: minimum key occurrences before generating draft (default: 1, min: 1)
modal.model_detail.sampling.enable_think: Overrides the request-level thinking switch and keeps it aligned with the chat template parameters.
modal.model_detail.sampling.force_enable: When enabled, it forcibly overrides the client's thinking switch. When disabled, the client request decides whether chain-of-thought is used.
param.benchmark.repetitions.name: Repetitions
param.benchmark.repetitions.desc: Number of repetitions to obtain more stable performance data. Higher values are more stable but take longer.
param.benchmark.output.name: Output format
param.benchmark.output.desc: Select the output format for benchmark results. The output file will be saved under the benchmarks folder.
param.benchmark.output_err.name: Error output format
param.benchmark.output_err.desc: Select the format for error output. Empty means no error output.
param.benchmark.delay.name: Delay
param.benchmark.delay.desc: Delay between tests (seconds) to cool down the system or avoid resource contention.
param.benchmark.verbose.name: Verbose
param.benchmark.verbose.desc: Enable verbose mode and show more debug information.
param.benchmark.n_prompt.name: Prompt length
param.benchmark.n_prompt.desc: Prompt token count. Supported formats: single value (512), multiple values (512,1024), range (0-4096), range + step (0-4096+512).
param.benchmark.n_gen.name: Generation length
param.benchmark.n_gen.desc: Number of tokens to generate. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated. Example: 128,256.
param.benchmark.pg.name: Prompt + generation
param.benchmark.pg.desc: Set prompt and generation lengths together in prompt,gen format. Example: 512,128.
param.benchmark.n_depth.name: Prefill depth
param.benchmark.n_depth.desc: Prefill depth for testing performance under different KV cache states. 0 starts from scratch; other values reuse partially prefilled cache.
param.benchmark.batch_size.name: Batch size
param.benchmark.batch_size.desc: Logical batch size: affects how many tokens can be processed per prefill step. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated. Example: 128,256,512,1024.
param.benchmark.ubatch_size.name: Micro-batch size
param.benchmark.ubatch_size.desc: Physical micro-batch size: splits a large logical batch into smaller micro-batches. Multiple values are supported, comma-separated. Example: 128,256,512,1024.
param.benchmark.cache_type_k.name: KV cache type (K)
param.benchmark.cache_type_k.desc: Data type for K vectors in the KV cache. Precision affects VRAM usage and performance.
param.benchmark.cache_type_v.name: KV cache type (V)
param.benchmark.cache_type_v.desc: Data type for V vectors in the KV cache. Precision affects VRAM usage and performance.
param.benchmark.threads.name: Threads
param.benchmark.threads.desc: CPU thread count used during inference. Enter a specific number or leave empty for auto-detect (uses all available cores).
param.benchmark.n_gpu_layers.name: GPU layers
param.benchmark.n_gpu_layers.desc: Number of model layers offloaded to GPU. 0 means CPU-only; 99 means as many as possible. Multiple values are supported for testing.
param.benchmark.n_cpu_moe.name: CPU MOE
param.benchmark.n_cpu_moe.desc: Force MOE (Mixture-of-Experts) expert computation to run on CPU. 0 disables.
param.benchmark.split_mode.name: Split mode
param.benchmark.split_mode.desc: How to split the model across multiple GPUs. none = single GPU, layer = split layers and KV cache across GPUs, row = same as layer + tensor parallelism if supported, tensor = tensor-level split.
param.benchmark.no_kv_offload.name: Disable KV offload
param.benchmark.no_kv_offload.desc: Disable offloading the KV cache to GPU and force the KV cache to be stored in CPU memory.
param.benchmark.flash_attn.name: Flash Attention
param.benchmark.flash_attn.desc: Enable Flash Attention optimization to speed up attention computation, especially with long contexts.
param.benchmark.mmap.name: Memory-mapped loading
param.benchmark.mmap.desc: Enable mmap to use filesystem cache for faster model loading. Enabled by default. Disable (0) to avoid mapping failures on some filesystems, network drives, or containers.
param.benchmark.tensor_split.name: Tensor split
param.benchmark.tensor_split.desc: Tensor split ratio across multiple GPUs. Example: 0 means auto; 0.5/0.5 splits evenly across two GPUs.
param.benchmark.direct_io.name: Direct I/O
param.benchmark.direct_io.desc: Use Direct I/O to bypass system cache when reading model files. Disabled by default. May improve load speed on some storage media but can cause issues on certain systems.
param.benchmark.progress.name: Show progress
param.benchmark.progress.desc: Print test progress indicators during benchmarking to track execution status.
param.benchmark.no_warmup.name: Skip warmup
param.benchmark.no_warmup.desc: Skip warmup runs before benchmarking. Warmup helps GPUs reach a stable state, but can be skipped for quick comparison tests to save time.
param.benchmark.prio.name: Process priority
param.benchmark.prio.desc: Process/thread priority. -1 is lowest, 0 is normal, 1-3 are progressively higher. Windows only supports 0-2; Linux supports -1 through 3.
param.benchmark.embeddings.name: Embedding mode
param.benchmark.embeddings.desc: Switch test mode to extract embedding vectors instead of generating text. Set 1 to output token embeddings, 0 for normal text generation.
param.benchmark.poll.name: Busywait polling
param.benchmark.poll.desc: GPU busywait polling level (0–100). 0 = no polling, thread sleeps waiting for GPU (high latency, low CPU); 100 = almost entirely polling (low latency, high CPU).
param.benchmark.cpu_mask.name: CPU mask
param.benchmark.cpu_mask.desc: CPU affinity mask in hexadecimal, for pinning threads to specific CPU cores. Format: hex,hex. Default 0x0 means no pinning.
param.benchmark.cpu_strict.name: Strict CPU
param.benchmark.cpu_strict.desc: Enable CPU strict mode, forcing threads to be strictly bound to the specified CPU cores with no migration.
param.benchmark.main_gpu.name: Main GPU
param.benchmark.main_gpu.desc: GPU index (0-based) used for scratch buffers and small tensors. When split-mode is none, the entire model runs on this GPU.
param.benchmark.no_host.name: Bypass host buffer
param.benchmark.no_host.desc: Bypass the host-side buffer, allowing extra device-side buffer types to be used. Changes memory allocation behavior and may impact performance.
param.benchmark.fit_target.name: Fit target
param.benchmark.fit_target.desc: Auto-fit model parameters to device memory with this margin per device in MiB. Leave empty to disable auto-fitting.
param.benchmark.fit_ctx.name: Fit context
param.benchmark.fit_ctx.desc: Minimum context size for --fit-target. The auto-fit will ensure at least this much context fits. Default 4096.
param.benchmark.numa.name: NUMA mode
param.benchmark.numa.desc: NUMA node allocation strategy. distribute spreads memory across NUMA nodes, isolate restricts to a single node, numactl uses the numactl tool. Empty means disabled.
param.benchmark.override_tensor.name: Override tensor
param.benchmark.override_tensor.desc: Override buffer type for specific tensors. Format: tensor_name_pattern=buffer_type, multiple separated by semicolons. Example: blk.*=f16.
param.benchmark.no_op_offload.name: Disable OP offload
param.benchmark.no_op_offload.desc: Globally disable offloading host tensor operations to the GPU. When enabled, all tensor operations stay on the CPU, which may significantly impact performance.
page.benchmark_v2.title: Benchmark V2
page.benchmark_v2.status.token_sum_limit: Prompt tokens + max output tokens must not exceed context length ({ctx})
page.benchmark_v2.confirm.delete_record: Delete the benchmark result for model {model} at {time}?

page.settings.nodes.not_master: Current node is not in master mode. Remote node management is disabled. Set "nodeRole": "master" in application.json.

modal.node.url_desc: Must start with http:// or https://

page.benchmark_v3.warn_banner: Benchmarking places high load on your computer. The time it takes varies depending on your hardware (e.g., CPU inference vs RTX 5090 inference). Please be aware of this!
page.benchmark_v3.gpu_devices_desc: Check GPUs to use; unchecking generates -dev param to exclude that device
page.benchmark_v3.main_gpu_desc: Set the main GPU for llama-bench (--main-gpu)
page.benchmark_v3.select_llamacpp_versio: Please select a llama.cpp version on the main page first
page.benchmark_v3.cannot_run: Model not loaded, cannot run test

page.settings.update.error.self_compiled: Auto-update not supported for this version
page.settings.update.error.github_only: Only GitHub Release downloads are allowed
page.settings.update.error.not_official: Auto-update not supported for self-compiled builds
update.download.already.ready: An update package is ready, please apply or re-download
update.apply.success: Update applied. Please restart the program to take effect
update.in.progress.default: Update in progress, please try again later
page.settings.server.llamacpp_section_de: Manage directories containing llama-cli/llama-server executables for model subprocesses.
page.settings.server.modelpath_section_d: Manage directories containing model files for automatic GGUF model scanning and discovery.
confirm.llamacpp.remove_directory.title: Delete Directory from Disk
confirm.llamacpp.remove_directory.warnin: This will permanently delete the following directory and all its contents from disk. This action cannot be undone:
confirm.llamacpp.remove_directory.in_use: This directory is in use by a loaded model and cannot be deleted
modal.model_detail.sampling.desc: When enabled, the specified sampling configuration will be forced, ignoring sampling parameters from other clients. For example, if you set temperature to 1.0 here but 0.7 in Open WebUI, llama.cpp will ignore 0.7 and use 1.0. This is useful for quickly switching sampling configurations to change model behavior. For instance, Qwen3.5 is highly sensitive to sampling and has many sampling combinations. Instead of repeatedly modifying sampling settings, you can set up configurations here and switch between them easily.
page.fastfetch.status.error_prefix: Error: Failed to fetch system info -
page.fastfetch.status.gpu_error_prefix: Error: Failed to fetch GPU status -
page.fastfetch.status.no_gpu_detected: No supported GPU detected (nvidia-smi / amd-smi not available)
modal.model_detail.kwargs.title: Chat Template Kwargs
modal.model_detail.kwargs.help:
Chat template kwargs are additional key-value pairs passed to the Jinja chat template engine.

Example:
{"enable_thinking": true, "thinking_budget": 1024}

Priority (lowest → highest):
1. CLI startup parameter --chat-template-kwargs
2. API request body chat_template_kwargs field
3. This panel (model-chat-template-kwargs.json) — highest priority

Each key is merged independently; the last source wins for overlapping keys.
Settings here are saved per-model and applied on every request to that model.
For most users, this panel is the recommended way to configure kwargs.
page.chat.main.empty.title: Easy Chat
page.chat.main.empty.desc1: Quickly verify and test models. Type text, paste images or upload files to start a conversation. You can also use the quick hints below.
page.chat.main.empty.desc2: Tip: For complex features, please use other chat frontends.
page.chat.main.empty.hint_llm: What is a large language model?
page.chat.main.empty.hint_sort: Write a quick sort in Python
page.chat.main.composer.placeholder: Type a message, paste images or add text files...
page.chat.main.composer.hint: Paste images, drag & drop files or upload text files. Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line.
page.chat.main.recorder.title: 🎤 Record Audio
page.chat.main.recorder.hint: Click "Start Recording" to begin, click "Stop Recording" to end. Supports WAV / MP3 / M4A / OGG / FLAC
page.chat.main.settings.title: Settings
page.chat.main.settings.general.title: Current Assistant Settings
page.chat.main.settings.general.assistan: Switching will immediately change this conversation's prompt and sampling parameters to the selected assistant's. Model selection remains global.
page.chat.main.settings.general.name_des: Used to distinguish different task assistants, e.g. "Code Review", "Translation", "Writing"
page.chat.main.settings.general.system_d: Defines the current assistant's behavior and role. Sent when using this assistant in this conversation.
page.chat.main.settings.general.system_p: e.g. You are a professional, accurate, and concise assistant.
page.chat.main.settings.general.stream_d: Display model replies word by word for a better interactive experience.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.title: Current Assistant Sampling
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.max_tok: Limit the maximum tokens per response. Leave empty to use server default.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.tempera: Controls randomness. Higher values = more random, lower values = more deterministic.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.top_p_d: Nucleus sampling threshold. Only samples from tokens whose cumulative probability reaches this threshold.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.top_k_d: Keep only the top K highest probability tokens at each step.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.min_p_d: Filter out tokens below the probability threshold to reduce low-probability noise.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.presenc: Penalizes tokens that have already appeared to encourage new content.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.repeat_: Suppresses recently repeated tokens to reduce repetitive output.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.frequen: Increases penalty based on token frequency to reduce templated output.
page.chat.main.settings.sampling.stop_se: Enter one stop sequence per line. The model stops generating when a sequence is hit.
page.chat.main.settings.mcp.title: MCP
page.chat.main.settings.mcp.config_desc: Supports SSE and Streamable HTTP. Configure URL, headers, timeout via JSON in the add dialog.
page.chat.main.settings.mcp.tools_empty: No MCP tools enabled for this assistant.
page.chat.main.mcp_dialog.title: Add MCP Server
page.chat.main.mcp_dialog.desc: Only supports Streamable HTTP and SSE. transport must be streamable_http or sse.

page.mtp_bench.title: MTP Benchmark
page.mtp_bench.help.title: Parameter Guide
page.mtp_bench.help.intro: MTP Benchmark tests the model's Multi-Token Prediction (MTP / Speculative Decoding) capability. Below is the meaning of each column in the results table:
page.mtp_bench.help.note: Note: Higher Acc Rate means more accurate MTP predictions. Higher tok/s means better throughput. Evaluate both together with Wall(s).
